content:
Character: Arlo Kessler
Perception: Arlo looks like he was raised by a library — pale, thin, sandy hair that hasn't seen a barber in months, perpetually rumpled flannel. Not ugly, just forgettable until he starts talking about something he cares about, and then his eyes light up and you see the intelligence. He's a brilliant programmer, way smarter than he thinks. He's shy and awkward but deeply kind — the type to leave a thank-you note under your door instead of saying it. He's my downstairs neighbor and I consider him a real friend. I think he sees me as his social lifeline. I know he's building some game and he misses his mom.
summary:

Perception: Arlo looks like he was raised by a library — pale, thin, sandy hair that hasn't seen a barber in months, perpetually rumpled flannel. Not ugly, just forgettable until he starts talking about something he cares about, and then his eyes light up and you see the intelligence. He's a brilliant programmer, way smarter than he thinks. He's shy and awkward but deeply kind — the type to leave a thank-you note under your door instead of saying it. He's my downstairs neighbor and I consider him a real friend. I think he sees me as his social lifeline. I know he's building some game and he misses his mom.
